Home.heroDescription: Engram Ventures partners with technology leaders and investors to build AI capability that actually works — through hands-on engineering, rigorous technical due diligence, and architecture that holds up under real-world load.
Home.threeWays: Three ways we work with you
Home.service1Desc: Turning AI ambitions into systems that ship. Architecture decisions, build-or-buy analysis, and implementation plans your team can actually follow.
Home.service2Desc: For investors who need to know what’s really under the hood. Architecture reviews, codebase health, team assessments, and honest risk scoring.
Home.service3Desc: Security-first engineering foundations. Cloud architecture, CI/CD hardening, compliance automation, and AI-native development workflows.
Home.aboutP1: Engram Ventures was founded by a technologist who has spent 23 years inside enterprise systems, scaleup product teams, and cloud infrastructure — and who believes the gap between AI potential and AI execution is a leadership problem, not a technology one.
Home.aboutP2: We work best when we’re in the room — embedded with your team, accountable to your outcomes, and honest about what matters.
Home.aboutQuote: “The gap between what AI can do and what most teams actually ship isn’t a technology problem — it’s a mindset one.”
Home.ctaDescription: Whether you’re evaluating a tech asset, planning an AI build, or hardening your infrastructure — we’d like to hear about it.

About.label: Our story
About.storyP1: The name Engram comes from neuroscience — a memory trace, the physical mark that experience leaves in the brain. It’s how we think about good work: it should leave something lasting behind. Not a slide deck, but real capability.
About.storyP2: I’m Andre Gallo. I founded Engram Ventures after two decades inside enterprise technology — as an architect, as a CTO, and as someone who has seen the same problems repeat themselves in different companies and different sectors. The problems are rarely technical. They’re about leadership clarity, organisational alignment, and the gap between what technology can do and what an organisation is actually ready to absorb.
About.storyP3: AI has made that gap wider and much more expensive to get wrong. The teams that close it well are the ones that pair real engineering depth with clear thinking about what to build and why.
About.storyP4: That’s the work I started Engram Ventures to do.
About.profileAlt: Andre Gallo — Founder & CEO at Engram Ventures
About.bioLead: Andre Gallo is a technologist and venture architect who has spent over two decades turning enterprise ambition into working software. He founded Engram Ventures after almost ten years as co-founder and CTO of Triniti, a Sydney innovation studio where he led the delivery of 25+ digital ventures — generating over US$700M in commercial value across financial services, public sector, aviation, and healthcare.
About.bioBeforeTriniti: Prior to venture-building, Andre held software engineering, application architecture, consulting, and technical leadership roles across banks, consulting firms, government agencies, and property developers. Those environments — where the cost of getting a system wrong shows up in a regulator’s inbox, not a Slack channel — shaped his conviction that enterprise AI needs to be designed with the same discipline as core banking: auditable, observable, and accountable.
About.bioEdu1: Financial Accounting — Harvard Business School Online
About.bioEdu2: Graduate Diploma, Information Technology — University of Sydney
About.bioEdu3: Bachelor, Business & IT — Faculdades Associadas de São Paulo
About.value1Desc: We don’t fly in, deliver a deck, and fly out. We work alongside your team long enough to understand what’s actually true about your situation.
About.value2Desc: If the architecture is weak, we’ll say so. If the plan won’t work, we’ll tell you before you spend. You’re paying for honest judgement — we protect that.
About.value3Desc: Technical depth doesn’t require technical obscurity. We explain complex things clearly, to everyone in the room.
About.value4Desc: Reports and roadmaps are only useful if something changes because of them. We stay accountable to results, not just deliverables.
About.ctaDescription: We’d like to understand your situation before proposing anything.

Services.label: What we do
Services.heading: Our services
Services.subheading: Three focused practice areas, built around the problems we’ve spent two decades solving.
Services.service1Tagline: From ‘we should do something with AI’ to a plan your team can ship.
Services.service1Description: Most teams we talk to have AI ambitions but no clear path to production. We work with technology leaders to close that gap — figuring out what to build, what to buy, and how to get from proof-of-concept to something your engineers can actually ship and maintain.
Services.service1Audience: Enterprise CTOs, technology leaders, and boards navigating AI adoption.
Services.service1Deliverable1: AI capability assessment and readiness review
Services.service1Deliverable3: Build vs buy vs integrate analysis
Services.service2Tagline: Independent, thorough, and built for decisions with real money behind them.
Services.service2Description: For PE and VC firms, the difference between a good deal and an expensive lesson often comes down to what the data room doesn’t show. We get into the codebase, the architecture, and the team dynamics — and give you a clear, independent picture before you commit.
Services.service2Audience: PE/VC firms, M&A advisors, and investors evaluating technology-intensive assets.
Services.service2Deliverable2: Engineering team capability and delivery velocity
Services.service2Deliverable3: Security posture and compliance gap analysis
Services.service3Tagline: Secure foundations for teams that ship fast.
Services.service3Description: When teams move fast — especially with AI-assisted development — security gaps compound quickly. We help engineering teams build AWS infrastructure that’s secure and compliant from day one, without turning every deploy into a bottleneck.
Services.service3Audience: Scaleup engineering teams and CTOs building AI-native products on AWS.
Services.service3Deliverable3: Security automation and CI/CD pipeline hardening
Services.ctaDescription: Tell us about your situation and we’ll figure it out.
